incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong category on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Look at the picture graph. How many symbols does Yellow Items have?

incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong category on second attempt

params.description: Yellow Items category highlights on picture graph.
Yellow Items bar highlights on bar graph.

steps.text: Look at Yellow Items. The picture graph shows 4 symbols. Each symbol stands for 10. The bar graph shows a bar to 40. Both show the same value.

incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong category three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Yellow Items category highlights on picture graph with confirmed animation.
Yellow Items bar highlights on bar graph with confirmed animation.

steps.text: Let me show you. Yellow Items on the picture graph has 4 symbols. Each symbol equals 10, so that is 40 total. Yellow Items on the bar graph reaches to 40 on the axis. Both graphs show 40 for Yellow Items. That is how we know they show the same data.
